content_markdown: A cell of e.m.f. $E$ and internal resistance $r$ is connected to a variable resistor. A voltmeter is connected so as to measure the potential difference across the terminals of the cell. Which **one** of the following is the correct circuit diagram of the arrangement?

diagram_metadata.description: Four circuit diagrams labeled A, B, C, and D showing different configurations of a cell (E), internal resistance (r), a voltmeter (V), and a variable resistor.

vibe_explanation: To measure the terminal potential difference of a cell, the voltmeter must be connected in parallel across the entire cell unit (which consists of the ideal e.m.f. source $E$ and the internal resistance $r$ in series). In diagram B, the voltmeter is connected across both the e.m.f. and the internal resistor, which are themselves in series with the external variable resistor. This correctly captures the voltage drop $V = E - Ir$ available to the external circuit.
